steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank falls at the end of the first independent clause: "...attempts to make use of the entire visual light [spectrum ___]." What follows is a participial phrase ("sporting rainbow-striped goggles") attached to a second independent clause. The blank must provide punctuation that correctly separates the first independent clause from the elaborating material that follows.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — spectrum, while: "While" introduces a subordinate clause, but what follows begins with a participial phrase, not a subject + verb. This creates a grammatical mismatch. ✗
  • Choice B — spectrum:: A colon correctly signals that what follows elaborates on or illustrates the preceding independent clause. The description of the rainbow-striped swimmer illustrates how the painting uses the full visual light spectrum. ✓
  • Choice C — spectrum: No punctuation leaves the first independent clause fused directly to the participial phrase and second clause — a run-on. ✗
  • Choice D — spectrum while: Same problem as A, but now without even a comma before "while." ✗
That is the correct answer.
